meta.name: Alffrin Regi
meta.tagline: Cyber-Physical Systems · Embedded AI · Autonomous Systems
meta.description: I am a Robotics Engineer focused on building intelligent robotic systems that combine advanced sensing, embedded AI, power electronics, and real-time control. I work across the full stack of robotics — integrating mechanical design, electronics, and intelligent software to create autonomous systems capable of operating in complex real-world environments.

projects.name: Disaster Response Drone
bullets: Developed an autonomous drone capable of human detection using Jetson Nano and optimized YOLO model for real-time edge inference in disaster environments
Integrated onboard vision processing and sensor fusion for stability and robust field deployment under degraded conditions
Designed the platform for disaster scenarios focusing on endurance, efficient hardware, and enhanced decision-making algorithms

caseStudy.problem: Search-and-rescue teams face dangerous, time-critical environments where manual drone operation is slow and error-prone. Existing UAVs lacked onboard intelligence for autonomous human detection without cloud dependency.
caseStudy.approach: Deployed a fine-tuned YOLOv5 model directly on a Jetson Nano with TensorRT optimization, achieving 12 FPS inference at the edge. Fused IMU + barometer + optical flow for stable hover in GPS-degraded environments.
caseStudy.result: Achieved reliable human detection at 15m altitude with <200ms latency. Successfully tested in simulated rubble environments. Reduced operator workload by ~70% in trials.
caseStudy.lessons: Edge inference optimization is as important as model accuracy. TensorRT INT8 quantization was the key to real-time performance on constrained hardware.

projects.name: Landmine Detection Rover
bullets: Developed a ground robot equipped with a custom GPR sensing pipeline for subsurface anomaly detection in hazardous terrain
Designed the full RF chain including LNA, couplers, attenuators, and signal conditioning hardware for mid-frequency Ground Penetrating Radar
Built a processing workflow using MATLAB and GPRPy to visualize underground signatures and support real-time field interpretation

caseStudy.problem: Landmine clearance in post-conflict zones is slow and lethal for human demarkers. Existing detection equipment is expensive and non-autonomous.
caseStudy.approach: Designed a custom 500MHz–1GHz GPR RF chain from first principles: LNA, directional couplers, bandpass filters, and a software-defined receiver. Mounted on a ROS2-controlled differential-drive rover with GPS logging.
caseStudy.result: Detected buried metallic and non-metallic targets at depths up to 30cm in sandy soil. Full system cost ~8× less than commercial equivalents.
caseStudy.lessons: Signal conditioning hardware quality dominates GPR performance more than algorithm choice. Shielding and antenna separation are critical to SNR.

projects.name: Cybersecurity LLM with Whisper Interface
bullets: Fine-tuned a lightweight LLM specializing in cybersecurity threat detection and analysis with automated response workflows for incident handling
Integrated Whisper for speech-based interaction, enabling voice-driven log queries on secure systems
Optimized for low-latency inference and secure on-device processing of sensitive data

caseStudy.problem: Security analysts spend hours parsing verbose system logs during incidents. Existing LLMs are too large for air-gapped secure environments and not specialized for cybersecurity.
caseStudy.approach: Fine-tuned a 7B parameter model on a curated cybersecurity corpus (CVE descriptions, attack patterns, log formats). Integrated OpenAI Whisper for hands-free voice queries. Quantized to 4-bit for deployment on a local workstation.
caseStudy.result: Reduced log triage time by ~60% in internal testing. Voice query latency <2s on CPU-only hardware. Model fit entirely within 8GB VRAM.
caseStudy.lessons: Domain-specific fine-tuning on a small model outperforms general-purpose large models for structured security tasks. Data curation quality trumps dataset size.

projects.name: Vision-Enabled Quadruped Robot
bullets: Built a quadruped robot capable of perception-based navigation using onboard computer vision and real-time inference for dynamic environments
Implemented multi-sensor fusion for environmental understanding and adaptive terrain locomotion

caseStudy.problem: Legged robots in research labs often rely on expensive centralized compute and structured environments. Real-world deployment demands low-cost, adaptive locomotion with onboard perception.
caseStudy.approach: Built a 12-DOF quadruped from scratch using 3D-printed structure and custom servo driver boards. Ran a simplified CPG-based gait controller on STM32, with ROS2 on Raspberry Pi 4 handling perception and high-level navigation.
caseStudy.result: Successfully navigated unstructured terrain including gravel and low steps. Gait stability maintained up to 8° tilt. Full BOM under $400.
caseStudy.lessons: Mechanical compliance matters as much as software control. Soft-mounting servos dramatically improved stability on rough terrain without any algorithm changes.
